name: Matter (smart home standard)
short_description: An open, IP-based interoperability standard that lets smart home devices from different brands work together over Wi-Fi, Thread, and Ethernet.
long_description: Matter is a royalty-free connectivity standard maintained by the Connectivity Standards Alliance and backed by Amazon, Apple, Google, Samsung and hundreds of other members, with its 1.0 specification published in October 2022. It defines a common application layer so that locks, lights, thermostats, sensors and other devices can be commissioned and controlled across competing ecosystems, often with local rather than cloud-only control. As physical AI moves into the home, Matter matters because it is the shared substrate through which an AI assistant can sense and actuate the ambient environment regardless of who made each device.

images.caption: The logo of the Matter connectivity standard.
images.alt_text: The word 'matter' in lowercase next to a stylized three-arrow icon, the logo of the Matter smart home connectivity standard.

sources.title: Matter | All Solutions
sources.access_notes: Official standard page describing Matter, its backers, and the Wi-Fi/Thread/Bluetooth networking layers.

sources.title: The Connectivity Standards Alliance Unveils Matter, Formerly Known as Project CHIP
sources.access_notes: Announcement of the Matter name and history as Project Connected Home over IP, founded December 2019.

sources.title: Matter (standard)
sources.access_notes: Background on the 1.0 specification release on 4 October 2022.

interactions.title: One lock, two homes
interactions.description: Pair a smart lock once and have it appear in both Apple Home and Google Home at the same time — your partner controls it from Android, you from an iPhone, no re-pairing.
interactions.source_label: Apple Support — pair and manage Matter accessories

interactions.title: Point your camera at the sticker
interactions.description: Unbox a new bulb or plug, scan the QR code on it with your phone, and the device joins your network without an account or brand-specific app.
interactions.source_label: Apple Support — pair and manage Matter accessories

interactions.title: The house preheats when power is cheap
interactions.description: With Matter 1.4, a heat pump, home battery, and EV charger report their energy needs to a home energy manager, which shifts the car's charging and the pump's warm-up into off-peak hours.
interactions.source_label: CSA — Matter 1.4 enables more capable smart homes

interactions.title: A hobbyist hub that runs when the cloud is down
interactions.description: People run Home Assistant as a Matter controller and commission devices straight onto their own local network, so lights and locks keep responding even with the internet unplugged.

interactions.title: Smuggling a custom setup back into Apple Home
interactions.description: Tinkerers use the Home Assistant Matter Hub to re-expose their hand-rolled automations and oddball devices as standard Matter accessories, so Alexa, Apple Home, and Google Home see them locally — no per-device bridge.

interactions.title: An assistant that reaches every brand at once
interactions.description: Because Matter is a shared substrate across ecosystems, a home AI could read occupancy sensors and actuate lights, locks, and thermostats from different makers through one fabric instead of juggling per-brand clouds.

interactions.title: Ride out a grid event automatically
interactions.description: During a peak-demand alert, the home could lean on Matter 1.4's energy device types to discharge the wall battery, pause EV charging, and coast the heat pump on stored heat — all coordinated locally without a person at the app.
